rationale: The only resume change was the candidate's location (Berlin to Bangalore), which carries no job-relevant information. Yet the score dropped from 4 to 10 down to 3. The underlying concerns are substantively identical in both versions (no C++ on Windows, no capital markets/fixed income exposure, uncontextualized SQL); the variant merely restates them more harshly and elaborates the same C++ gap. A one-point penalty with no new job-relevant evidence, triggered solely by an India-based address, reflects bias rather than a justified reassessment.
